summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a classic May‑20‑1973 UCSB show with pristine audio and standout jams like Playing In The Band and Eyes Of The World
review: The Charlie Miller transfer delivers crystal‑clear, virtually flawless soundboard quality that lets the magic of this late‑May ’73 California summer gig shine through.  Highlights include a soaring 19‑minute Playing In The Band, an emotive Eyes Of The World and a fiery Other One/Nobody’s Jam stretch, while the band’s energy only builds as the night progresses.

ai_rating.rationale: Over a dozen enthusiastic reviews praise both the performance (standout jams, solid third set) and the pristine soundboard recording; only minor technical hiccups are noted, leading to an overall rating near the top of the scale.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Praised as pristine, crisp and ‘stunning’; Charlie Miller’s transfer is repeatedly called top‑shelf. A few reviewers mention minor tech glitches, but overall clarity and balance are superb.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The concert builds nicely, with a solid first set that tightens up into an explosive second and a third set hailed as the highlight; reviewers note the show gets better as it goes, delivering classic ’73 energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His raspy 1973 voice is noted as unique; reviewers love his passionate singing even when battling a cold and praise his guitar work throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as ‘fired up’ with strong bass presence, especially in the third‑set jams.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard role is praised; Donna’s harmonies are called angelic. Lots of Keith/Donna moments noted.
